---
title: "1.6 Basic workflow"
---

## Explanation

Use this workflow for most subsections:

1. Read the short explanation.
2. Inspect the concrete syntax or example.
3. Look up unfamiliar terms.
4. Solve the exercise by hand.
5. Use Julia only when a small coding check helps.
6. If you ask an AI agent to generate an answer, treat it as a worked example, not as something to copy.
7. Understand the generated answer in detail: every line of code, every formula, every assumption, and every conclusion.
8. If anything is unclear, ask the AI agent follow-up questions until you can explain it in your own words.
9. If the AI agent edits files, review its reasoning and any diff it creates.

## Things to look up

- Hand-written exercise
- Pseudocode
- Unit test
- Validation
- Reproducibility
- Worked example

## Exercise

For one subsection, write a short study plan that includes reading, searching, a hand-written answer, optional AI help, and an optional Julia check.

If your plan uses AI-generated answers, also write how you will check that you understand the answer in detail.

## Notes for the exercise

- Do not skip the hand-written step.
- Do not start by asking an AI agent for the final answer.
- If you later ask an AI agent for an answer, inspect it line by line.
- Ask follow-up questions about every unclear term, formula, code line, or assumption.
- Do not submit or accept an answer that you cannot explain without looking at the AI output.
- Make the validation step explicit.
